data_source: FDA-approved Prescribing Information — Merck & Co. (DailyMed / FDA label, current as of 2025)
source_note: Keytruda (pembrolizumab) has the most expansive oncology label in FDA history — 40+ approved indications. This document captures the core brand intelligence layer. Full label at DailyMed: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=keytruda

brand_metadata.drug_class: Anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody / PD-1 blocker / Immune checkpoint inhibitor
brand_metadata.approval_breadth: 40+ tumor type indications across adult and pediatric populations

approved_tumor_types.indication_text: Unresectable or metastatic melanoma; adjuvant treatment of melanoma with lymph node involvement following complete resection.
First-line treatment of metastatic NSCLC with PD-L1 expression (TPS ≥1%) as monotherapy or in combination with chemotherapy; second-line for PD-L1 TPS ≥1% post-platinum progression.
approved_tumor_types.biomarker_required: PD-L1 TPS ≥1% for some regimens; TPS ≥50% for monotherapy first-line

approved_tumor_types.tumor_type: Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma (HNSCC)
approved_tumor_types.indication_text: First-line treatment of metastatic or unresectable, recurrent HNSCC with PD-L1 CPS ≥1 as monotherapy or in combination with platinum and FU chemotherapy.
Relapsed or refractory cHL in adults and pediatric patients.

Locally advanced or metastatic urothelial carcinoma in patients who are not eligible for cisplatin-containing chemotherapy and whose tumors express PD-L1 (CPS ≥10), or any platinum ineligible patient; also second-line post-platinum.
approved_tumor_types.biomarker_required: PD-L1 CPS ≥10 for cisplatin-ineligible first-line monotherapy

approved_tumor_types.indication_text: Adult and pediatric patients with unresectable or metastatic TMB-H (≥10 mut/Mb) solid tumors who have progressed on prior treatment with no satisfactory alternatives. Also MSI-H or dMMR solid tumors.
approved_tumor_types.note: This is a tumor-agnostic approval — one of the first in oncology history

approved_tumor_types.indication_text: First-line treatment of locally advanced unresectable or metastatic HER2-positive gastric or GEJ adenocarcinoma in combination with trastuzumab and chemotherapy.
First-line treatment of locally advanced or metastatic esophageal or GEJ cancer with PD-L1 CPS ≥10 in combination with platinum and fluoropyrimidine chemotherapy; also second-line monotherapy.
Persistent, recurrent, or metastatic cervical cancer with PD-L1 expression (CPS ≥1) in combination with chemotherapy with or without bevacizumab.
Second-line or later treatment of HCC in patients previously treated with sorafenib.
Recurrent locally advanced or metastatic Merkel cell carcinoma.
First-line treatment of advanced RCC in combination with axitinib; also in combination with lenvatinib for first-line advanced RCC.
Advanced endometrial carcinoma not MSI-H or dMMR in combination with lenvatinib in patients who have progressed on prior systemic therapy. Also MSI-H/dMMR endometrial carcinoma as monotherapy.

High-risk, early-stage TNBC in combination with chemotherapy (neoadjuvant), then as single agent adjuvant; locally recurrent unresectable or metastatic TNBC with PD-L1 (CPS ≥10) in combination with chemotherapy.
First-line treatment of unresectable or metastatic MSI-H or dMMR CRC.
Locally advanced unresectable or metastatic BTC in combination with gemcitabine and cisplatin.

indications.selection_note: Above covers major indications. Full label lists additional regimens and populations — always consult current PI for complete indication language.

mechanism_of_action.moa_summary: Pembrolizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody that binds to the PD-1 receptor on T-cells and blocks the interaction with PD-L1 and PD-L2 ligands. This releases PD-1 pathway-mediated inhibition of immune response, including anti-tumor immune response.
key_mechanisms: Blocks PD-1 / PD-L1 and PD-1 / PD-L2 interactions
Releases T-cell inhibition — restores anti-tumor immune response
IgG4/kappa isotype with Fc region modification to minimize Fc-mediated effector function
mechanism_of_action.biomarker_relevance: PD-L1 expression (CPS or TPS by IHC 22C3 pharmDx) is required for patient selection in many indications. TMB-H and MSI-H/dMMR are additional biomarker-driven selection criteria.

approved_claims.claim_text: KEYTRUDA is approved for more than 40 indications across more than 20 different tumor types — the broadest oncology label in FDA history.
approved_claims.note: Substantiated by FDA approval history; always state current exact number as approvals continue

approved_claims.claim_text: KEYTRUDA improved overall survival vs. chemotherapy in previously untreated metastatic NSCLC with PD-L1 TPS ≥50% (KEYNOTE-024).
approved_claims.supporting_data: KEYNOTE-024: median OS 26.3 months vs 14.2 months; HR 0.62 [95% CI: 0.48–0.81]

approved_claims.claim_text: KEYTRUDA + chemotherapy improved overall survival in first-line metastatic NSCLC with PD-L1 TPS ≥1% regardless of histology (KEYNOTE-189 / KEYNOTE-407).
approved_claims.supporting_data: KEYNOTE-189 (non-squamous): HR 0.56 [95% CI: 0.45–0.70]; KEYNOTE-407 (squamous): HR 0.71 [95% CI: 0.58–0.88]

approved_claims.claim_text: KEYTRUDA monotherapy demonstrated durable responses in advanced melanoma in patients who had not received prior therapy (KEYNOTE-006).
approved_claims.supporting_data: KEYNOTE-006: OS HR 0.68 vs ipilimumab; 5-year OS rate 38% with pembrolizumab

approved_claims.claim_text: KEYTRUDA is approved for treatment of TMB-High solid tumors regardless of tumor histology — a tissue-agnostic approval.
approved_claims.supporting_data: KEYNOTE-158, ORR 29% in TMB-H patients across 10 cohorts

approved_claims.claim_text: KEYTRUDA improved event-free survival in early-stage TNBC when added to neoadjuvant chemotherapy (KEYNOTE-522).

contraindications.text: No absolute contraindications listed in the label per se, but pembrolizumab should not be used in patients with active, severe autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (clinical judgment required).
contraindications.note: This is a clinical caution, not a formal FDA contraindication — consult current label

immune_mediated_adverse_reactions.overview: KEYTRUDA can cause severe and fatal immune-mediated adverse reactions (irAEs). These may involve any organ system and typically occur during treatment or after treatment discontinuation.
categories: Immune-mediated endocrinopathies (hypophysitis, thyroid disorders, adrenal insufficiency, T1DM)
Immune-mediated dermatologic reactions (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis)
immune_mediated_adverse_reactions.management_principle: Withhold or permanently discontinue based on severity; treat with corticosteroids as appropriate. Hormone replacement for endocrinopathies.

standard_regimens: 2 mg/kg (up to 200 mg) IV Q3W for pediatric patients
dosing.administration: Intravenous infusion over 30 minutes. Do not administer as IV push or bolus.
dosing.duration: Until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or maximum 24 months for adjuvant/certain indications

required_companion_diagnostics.metric: CPS (Combined Positive Score) or TPS (Tumor Proportion Score) depending on tumor type

mandatory_references: All efficacy claims must reference specific KEYNOTE trial number and patient population
PD-L1 cutoff must be clearly stated when relevant to the approved indication
OS data should reference hazard ratio and confidence interval, not just median values
prohibited_claims: Do NOT use '40+ indications' without updating to current approved count
Do NOT claim efficacy in tumor types without the required biomarker selection unless biomarker-unselected data exists
Do NOT imply irAEs are mild — fatal cases have been reported across categories
required_fair_balance: Immune-mediated adverse reactions (multiple categories, some fatal)
Embryo-fetal toxicity (advise patients of reproductive potential)
